import docx
from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Pt, Inches, RGBColor
from docx.enum.text import WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH
from docx.enum.style import WD_STYLE_TYPE

doc = Document\(\)

# -- Style tweaks --
style = doc.styles['Normal']
font = style.font
font.name = 'Calibri'
font.size = Pt\(11\)
style.paragraph_format.space_after = Pt\(6\)

# Title
title = doc.add_heading\('How to Launch the Dashboards Using Codex', level=1\)
title.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER

# Subtitle
sub = doc.add_paragraph\(\)
sub.alignment = WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.CENTER
run = sub.add_run\('Aytm x Neo Smart Living Prototype — CPP AI Hackathon 2026'\)
run.font.size = Pt\(12\)
run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor\(100, 100, 100\)

doc.add_paragraph\(\)

# What is Codex?
doc.add_heading\('What Is Codex?', level=2\)
doc.add_paragraph\(
    'Codex is a coding assistant built into ChatGPT. It can read your project files, '
    'run terminal commands, and launch apps — all from a chat interface. '
    'You don\\'t need to know how to code to use it.'
\)

# Prerequisites
doc.add_heading\('Before You Start', level=2\)
p = doc.add_paragraph\(\)
p.add_run\('You need a ChatGPT account with Codex access.'\).bold = True
p.add_run\(' Codex is available with ChatGPT Plus, Team, and Enterprise plans.'\)
doc.add_paragraph\(
    'Make sure you have downloaded the prototype folder to your computer '
    '\(the folder containing dashboard.py, interview_dashboard.py, etc.\).'
\)

# Steps
doc.add_heading\('Step-by-Step Instructions', level=2\)

# Step 1
doc.add_heading\('Step 1: Open Codex and Upload the Project', level=3\)
items = [
    'Go to chatgpt.com and log in.',
    'Click "Codex" in the left sidebar \(or go to chatgpt.com/codex\).',
    'Click "Upload folder" and select the prototype folder.',
    'Wait for Codex to finish scanning the files.',
]
for item in items:
    doc.add_paragraph\(item, style='List Number'\)

# Step 2
doc.add_heading\('Step 2: Launch a Dashboard', level=3\)
doc.add_paragraph\('In the Codex chat box, type one of these prompts:'\)

# Prompt box 1
p = doc.add_paragraph\(\)
p.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches\(0.5\)
run = p.add_run\('launch the app'\)
run.font.name = 'Consolas'
run.font.size = Pt\(11\)
run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor\(0, 100, 0\)

doc.add_paragraph\(
    'Codex will automatically detect the Streamlit dashboards, install dependencies, '
    'and start the app. You can also be more specific:'
\)

prompts = [
    \('Survey dashboard:', 'launch dashboard.py'\),
    \('Interview dashboard:', 'launch interview_dashboard.py'\),
]
for label, prompt in prompts:
    p = doc.add_paragraph\(\)
    p.paragraph_format.left_indent = Inches\(0.5\)
    run = p.add_run\(label + '  '\)
    run.bold = True
    run = p.add_run\(prompt\)
    run.font.name = 'Consolas'
    run.font.size = Pt\(11\)
    run.font.color.rgb = RGBColor\(0, 100, 0\)

# Step 3
doc.add_heading\('Step 3: Open the Dashboard', level=3\)
items = [
    'After ~20-30 seconds, Codex will show a message saying "App is running."',
    'Click the blue link \(http://localhost:8501\) that appears in the chat.',
    'The dashboard will open in your browser.',
]
for item in items:
    doc.add_paragraph\(item, style='List Number'\)

# What the dashboards show
doc.add_heading\('What Do the Dashboards Show?', level=2\)

table = doc.add_table\(rows=3, cols=2\)
table.style = 'Light Shading Accent 1'
hdr = table.rows[0].cells
hdr[0].text = 'Dashboard'
hdr[1].text = 'What It Shows'
for cell in hdr:
    for paragraph in cell.paragraphs:
        for run in paragraph.runs:
            run.bold = True

row1 = table.rows[1].cells
row1[0].text = 'dashboard.py\\n\(Survey Insights\)'
row1[1].text = 'Quantitative survey results from 60 synthetic respondents — purchase intent, barrier analysis, concept appeal, demographic breakdowns, and segment comparisons.'

row2 = table.rows[2].cells
row2[0].text = 'interview_dashboard.py\\n\(Interview Insights\)'
row2[1].text = 'Qualitative interview analysis from 30 synthetic interviews — key themes, sentiment analysis, persona-level quotes, and cross-question patterns.'

# Troubleshooting
doc.add_heading\('Troubleshooting', level=2\)

issues = [
    \('"Codex can\\'t find the files"',
     'Make sure you uploaded the entire prototype folder, not just individual files.'\),
    \('"Module not found" error',
     'Ask Codex: "install the requirements and try again." It will run pip install -r requirements.txt automatically.'\),
    \('"NLTK data missing" error',
     'Ask Codex: "download the NLTK data and relaunch." It will run the download command for you.'\),
    \('Dashboard won\\'t load in browser',
     'Try clicking the link again, or ask Codex: "what port is the app running on?" and open that URL manually.'\),
    \('Want to switch dashboards',
     'Ask Codex: "stop the current app and launch interview_dashboard.py instead."'\),
]
for title_text, fix in issues:
    p = doc.add_paragraph\(\)
    run = p.add_run\(title_text\)
    run.bold = True
    p.add_run\(' — ' + fix\)

# Tips
doc.add_heading\('Tips', level=2\)
tips = [
    'You can ask Codex follow-up questions like "explain what this chart means" or "export this data as CSV."',
    'If something breaks, just say "fix it" — Codex will usually diagnose and resolve the issue.',
    'The sample data is pre-loaded in the output/ folder, so you don\\'t need an API key to explore the dashboards.',
]
for tip in tips:
    doc.add_paragraph\(tip, style='List Bullet'\)

# Save
output_path = "/Users/yufanlin/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-lyf918918@gmail.com/My Drive/CPP Teaching/Gen AI for Marketing Strategy/Term Project/AI Hackathon/Share/Aytm x Neo Smart Living/prototype/How_to_Launch_Dashboards_with_Codex.docx"
doc.save\(output_path\)
print\(f"Saved to: {output_path}"\)
PYEOF)
